name: collections
context: Install, list, verify, and scaffold Ansible collections so required modules and plugins are present
info: Collections are Ansible's distribution unit for modules, plugins, roles, and playbooks. This skill covers the operational collection lifecycle an autonomous agent actually needs: install exact collection versions, consume a `requirements.yml`, verify signed or expected content, inspect installed collections, and scaffold a new collection namespace with `ansible-galaxy`. It matters because playbooks fail fast when required collections are missing or unexpectedly upgraded, and because version pinning plus verification is the difference between reproducible automation and environmental drift on the control node.
body:
# Ansible Collections

Collections package namespaced content such as modules, plugins, roles, and even playbooks. The control node needs the right collections installed before [[ansible/playbooks]] or [[ansible/ad-hoc]] can resolve fully qualified module names like `community.general.ufw` or `amazon.aws.ec2_instance`.

## Install and Pin Versions

```bash
ansible-galaxy collection install community.general
ansible-galaxy collection install 'amazon.aws:>=8.0.0,<9.0.0'
```

## Install from requirements.yml

```yaml
collections:
  - name: community.general
    version: '>=10.0.0,<11.0.0'
  - name: amazon.aws
    version: '>=8.0.0,<9.0.0'
```

```bash
ansible-galaxy collection install -r collections/requirements.yml
```

## Inspect and Verify

```bash
ansible-galaxy collection list
ansible-galaxy collection verify community.general
```

## Scaffold a New Collection

```bash
ansible-galaxy collection init acme.platform
```

This creates the standard namespace layout for distributing modules, plugins, roles, and docs.

sections.title: Install and pin collection versions
sections.body: By default, `ansible-galaxy` installs the latest available version of a collection. For reproducible automation, pin versions or version ranges, especially in CI and long-lived playbook repos. Version drift on the control node is a common cause of module-not-found or behavior-change failures.

sections.title: requirements.yml is the team contract
sections.body: Use a `requirements.yml` file to declare every collection the project needs. This makes bootstrapping deterministic for local runs, CI workers, and ephemeral agent environments. Commit the file next to the playbooks that depend on it and install from it before execution.

sections.title: List and verify installed content
sections.body: Use `ansible-galaxy collection list` to see what is present on the control node and `collection verify` when you need higher confidence that a collection artifact matches expectations. This is especially relevant on long-lived runners or shared automation hosts where state may drift over time.

sections.title: Scaffold collections for reusable automation
sections.body: Use `ansible-galaxy collection init namespace.name` to create the package structure for your own reusable automation content. Collections are the right boundary when modules, roles, or plugins should be versioned and distributed beyond one repository. They are broader than a role and more reusable than copying task files between projects.

links.context: Playbooks depend on installed collections for module and plugin resolution
Use the module-resolution checklist when collections are missing or stale
Ad-hoc commands can call modules shipped by external collections
Missing collections and plugin resolution errors are common failure modes
Collection source and requirements files usually live in Git

related.note: Collections supply modules and plugins that playbooks execute
Versioned requirements and collection source code are typically managed in Git
The control node itself is commonly installed and maintained with Python tooling

scripts.name: Manage Collections
scripts.description: Install, list, verify, or scaffold Ansible collections with ansible-galaxy

ANSIBLE_GALAXY_ACTION.description: Collection action: install, list, verify, or init

ANSIBLE_COLLECTION_NAME.description: Collection name such as community.general or namespace.name

ANSIBLE_GALAXY_TOKEN.description: Galaxy API token for private or authenticated servers

ANSIBLE_FORCE.description: Set to true to overwrite existing content during install or init

collectionsPath.description: Target or lookup path for installed collections

force.description: Force overwrite on install or init

command.description: The ansible-galaxy command that was executed

author: dojo-community
